description: With 'wheli', any member of the available set can satisfy the phrase. 'soli' (SOME) asserts an unspecified nonempty subset; 'wheli' makes no prior selection. The predicate still says what the eventual choice may or must do.
articulatory_notes: Shape 'wh' with rounded lips and a high tongue dorsum, but leave the vocal folds still while air passes. Unround to 'e'; bring the tongue forward while you open the jaw halfway. Next, the blade touches the gum ridge behind the front teeth; voice escapes laterally before sliding into high-front unrounded 'i'. Accent 'whe'.
sound_symbolism: The voiceless rounded opening releases into the light lateral movement of 'li'. That easy passage can suggest a choice left unobstructed.
usage_notes: 'wheli' comes directly before an unmarked noun because it supplies the phrase's whole quantity strategy. In an offer or permission, it states that the speaker has left the selection open.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: During an offer, 'wheli' makes genuine flexibility audible. The listener does not have to hunt for an unstated preferred choice.
